title: Aurora ESPHome External Component Profile
description: Profile for a community ESPHome external_components module (a custom integration installed via the YAML 'external_components:' block, typically sourced from a GitHub repository). NOT to be confused with the 'external_components' field on a component profile, which describes hardware accessories. Entries should only be added after manual verification — the maintainer's last activity, that the source URL is live, and that the listed chips are actually supported. The catalog is intentionally narrow; the validator side handles unknown components without requiring a catalog entry.

external_component_id.description: Stable snake_case identifier (matches the ESPHome `components:` key when included).

summary.description: One-sentence description of what the component does. Plain English, no marketing copy.

ref.description: Branch, tag, or commit. null means HEAD/main.

path.description: Subdirectory inside the repository where the component lives.

max_known_version.description: Highest ESPHome version that has been observed to work. null means no upper bound observed.

yaml_example.description: Inline ESPHome YAML snippet that imports and uses the component. Used by Volt as a starting point.

datasheet_url.description: When the component drives a specific hardware sensor or actuator, link to the hardware datasheet so Volt can cross-check pin and voltage requirements.

last_active_check_date.description: ISO 8601 date the maintainer's recent activity was last verified. Triggers a re-verification if older than 6 months.

responsive.description: Whether the maintainer typically responds to issues within a reasonable window. Soft signal.

successor.description: external_component_id or ESPHome core component name that replaces this one.

warnings.description: Known caveats: 'requires platformio override', 'incompatible with arduino framework', etc. Surfaced verbatim to the user when Volt picks the component.

verified_by.description: Record of who manually verified the entry against the source repository and an actual install. At least one entry required for any non-experimental lifecycle status.

last_verified.description: Most recent date any field in the profile was confirmed against the upstream source.
